campaigns.title: Help provide shelter, food, clothes and medical support to abandoned widow mothers of Vrindavan. Donate now
campaigns.description: In Vrindavan, thousands of elderly widow mothers face the harsh reality of abandonment and suffering. With no one to turn to, many are left begging on the streets, exposed to heat, cold and rain,Donate so that Maitri can provide shelter, food, clothes and medical support to abandoned elderly mothers now. Give generously.Winnie Singh has witnessed many cases of widow mothers being abandoned on the streets of Vrindavan, one of the holiest places in India. “Children leave their mothers as soon as they become widows. The children start feeling the burden. This has become a practice in today's society that money comes first and everything else later,” says Winnie whose Maitri India, rescues widowed mothers from the streets of Vrindavan.Many widowed elderly mothers often end up living a lonely life and begging to survive on the streets. And Maitri aspires to take care of every widow in need of help, but because of lack of funds, Winnie and Maitri are in a position to do it."Elderly widows don't have shelter. They sleep in cowsheds over sacks. They don't have a blanket. Nights are really difficult for them," shares Winnie Singh. Maitri aims to:Provide shelter and nutritious meals leading to improved health and wellbeingMake physical and mental health services accessibleOffer necessary quality aged care and assisted living supportIncrease awareness and engagement around the struggles of abandoned widow mothersWhy donate to MaitriWinnie needs your support to help those in need of food, healthcare and well-being. “Maitri urgently needs your support to provide nutritious food, clothing and healthcare to sustain these widow mothers who are prone to more illnesses as they age,” appeals Winnie. “You can become the family they were denied. Hold their hands so they don't need to beg ever again,” she adds. Let’s rescue abandoned widows mothers from the streets of Vrindavan and give them a better future. Donate now.Our help will enable Maitri to save hundreds of abandoned widows from a life of begging, and restore the respect, dignity, and identity they deserve.

campaigns.title: Help feed and care for elderly parents abandoned by their own families
campaigns.description: Vijaya was around 70 years old and a street in Avadi, Chennai was her home. Abandoned by family, she had no roof to call home and no means of survival. The police had warned her to move on. Residents had complained. Nobody wanted to touch what they saw as somebody else's problem. Then someone called Anbagam. The rescue team from the NGO arrived. There was a complication: Vijaya refused to leave without her puppies. The team found homes for each one. Then they brought Vijaya in. Today she is healthy, comfortable, and happy. This is what Anbagam does. It meets people exactly where they are, asks nothing of them except to come, and gives them something most of us take entirely for granted: a safe place to be.But without your support this safe haven for the elderly might not even exist. Be the pillar of support this old age home needs. Donate generously!Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).A life-long missionSince 1999, Mohammed Rafi, a small businessman from Parry's in Chennai, has been doing this work. He started by housing people in his own home. When that filled up, he built more. Today, Anbagam runs three homes across Chennai and Thiruvallur, caring for 400 residents: mentally ill, elderly, abandoned, forgotten.Running this for 400 people costs money. Food alone costs roughly Rs 4 lakh every month. Medicines and psychiatric care cost approximately Rs 1 lakh a month. Add staff, a doctor, cleaning, bedding, and clothing, and the annual operating cost across all three homes comes to around Rs 77 lakh.Your contribution means a warm meal, a comfortable bed and necessary medical treatment for an abandoned elderly grandparent. Support Now!Impact beyond numbersOver 7,000 people have walked through Anbagam's doors. Around 3,000 have been reunited with families across India. The rest stay, because Anbagam is the family they have now.Mohammed Rafi said it simply: “Share your happiness with the people who are here. Help them!”Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s most trusted online donation platform, to support Anbagam, in its mission to give abandoned elderly parents the care they deserve.

campaigns.title: Don’t let children fight cancer on an empty stomach, help them with nutrition. Donate now
campaigns.description: “My 4-year-old son was diagnosed with an aggressive form of leukaemia. We brought him to Mumbai 6 months back when he was fighting for life. But within a couple of months, Vidhan’s condition improved drastically. Apart from the chemotherapy, one thing that has had a significant impact on his health is the nutrition support provided by Cuddles Foundation,” says Meenakshi Motiwala, who hails from Dhule, Maharashtra. Meenakshi and her husband had no means of supporting Vidhan with special nutrition, but thanks to the NGO, their son is in better shape.Cuddles Foundation is a nonprofit that helps hundreds of children fight cancer each year, but there are thousands more still battling cancer and in need of help. Help the NGO extend its reach and save many more lives. Donate now.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India)Non-profit organisation Cuddles Foundation has been on a mission to give underprivileged children having cancer a fighting chance against the disease with complete nutritional support. Giving kids battling cancer a fighting chanceDoctors often say that proper nutrition is the first line of treatment for children fighting cancer. However, because of poor financial background, many parents can’t give their kids life-saving nutrition and often witness the condition deteriorate before their eyes. “We have observed that there is a marked improvement or maintenance in the health and nutritional status of more than 80% of children with cancer by giving them proper meals. Nutritional support can not only give a child the strength needed to go through chemotherapy, but also help in reducing the side effects of cancer treatment significantly. Sadly, the life-saving nutrition needed to help children defeat cancer is well beyond the reach for many parents due to their financial condition,” says Purnota Dutta Bahl, founder of the nonprofit. Purnota embarked upon the mission to save the lives of children with cancer over a decade back after a chance visit to the Tata Memorial Hospital. “I saw a baby girl who back then, was about the same age as my daughter. The girl was fighting cancer and was visibly malnourished. I was informed by the nurse that children get treatment for cancer at the hospital free of cost, but their condition keeps deteriorating as their parents can’t afford the required nutrition. It was on that day that I decided to at least give these children a fighting chance against cancer,” explains the founder of the nonprofit organisation. Donate to heal thousands more“We have been able to take our programme to over 50 hospitals, spread across 17 states. But still there are thousands of children we are yet to reach. The cost of providing nutritional support to a child and their family suffering from cancer is about ₹80,000 per year (or ₹7,000 a month). Cancer is absolutely curable. A little love and support from you can help children suffering from cancer complete their treatment and go on to live functional and productive lives in the future,” says Purnota.Donate to this fundraiser by give.do, India’s largest fundraising platform, to support Cuddles Foundation in their mission to give life-saving nutrition to children suffering from cancer and give them the strength to fight cancer.

campaigns.title: Help Leena in saving girls from being lost forever in red-light areas
campaigns.description: "Hundreds of young girls are lost forever in the dark lanes of red-light areas," says Leena Kejriwal, the founder of Missing Link Trust. These girls endure unimaginable abuse and exploitation, being lured or trafficked into an industry that breaks them physically and emotionally. Missing Link Trust is on a mission to prevent the tragedy before it happens, and also rehabilitate girls and women who are rescued from forced sex work.Leena’s journey began with her powerful street art project in Kolkata, where she painted haunting silhouettes of missing girls to raise awareness against sex trafficking. Now, she leads a larger fight — one that focuses on prevention and rehabilitation. But this mission needs your support.You can save countless girl children from going missing forever. Donate now.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).Preventing Trafficking Before It Begins“When girls come into a red-light area, the first thing is that they are broken, physically and emotionally,” Leena says. “It’s like they will be caught, hands and legs tied, and all heinous crimes committed on them.” These are the brutal realities that Missing Link Trust fights against every day.Many of these girls are from impoverished regions like the Sundarbans, and traffickers prey on their vulnerability. “It’s shocking that they sell themselves for as low as a hundred rupees,” Leena adds. "They ready her through violent acts, removing all threads of shame from her."But why wait until a girl is trafficked to save her? Missing Link Trust believes in stopping trafficking at its root — through “prevention, prevention, prevention” as Leena puts it. The goal is simple: protect girls from ever being lost to this darkness.A Safe World for Children“Our mission is the prevention of abuse and exploitation of children,” Leena emphasizes. “Our vision is to create a safe world for children, and we want to do this with a preventive methodology.”Every Donation MattersYour support will help Missing Link Trust continue its vital work of rescuing, rehabilitating, and preventing the trafficking of young girls. The funds raised will go directly towards:Prevention programs in vulnerable areas to stop trafficking before it happens.Rehabilitation services for girls rescued from red-light areas, providing them with medical care, emotional support, and education.Awareness campaigns to reach at-risk communities and ensure girls are protected from traffickers.Together, We Can Make a Difference“Why wait for a girl to get trafficked to save her?” asks Leena. With your help, we can stop this horror before it begins. Your donation will help us create a world where no girl is lost to the nightmare of trafficking.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest and most trusted online giving platform, to support Missing Link Trust to save young girls from being trafficked and help rehabilitate those rescued.

campaigns.title: Help abandoned grandparents fight hunger and receive medical care. Donate now
campaigns.description: 65-year-old Shakuntala completely depends on our partner NGO, Share and Care, for her food requirements, as she has no one else to take care of her. “When my only child was young, my husband Nadesan died. It was just me and my young son. After my son got married, he left me to stay with his in-laws, and I was alone. I am old, have pain in my legs, knees, shoulders and my entire body so I cannot work as I once did. But the food rations such as the nuts, lentils, wheat flour and rice I receive from Share and Care help me tremendously and I can eat well every day,” she says. Share and Care helps hundreds of abandoned grandparents, most of them being elderly women. The senior citizens belong to tribal communities and have limited access to necessities such as food and medicines to stay alive and well. This Shivratri,  you can help with food and other essentials. Support them. Donate now. Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India), & 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code (USA). Abandoned elderly supported by Share and Care400 elderly parents abandoned by their children have no one to take care of them and need your help. They suffer from hunger, sight and hearing failure, diabetes, high blood pressure and other medical conditions. As they cannot work and support themselves because of old age, they cannot afford medicines or even clothing. So, they remain poor and alone. Share and Care’s mission Since 1984, Share and Care has been caring for disadvantaged groups of people, including elderly parents who have been living in poverty after being abandoned by their children. The founders, a couple named Carmel and Arokiasamy, aim to eliminate poverty and the suffering of the poor through social welfare programs and education. To achieve this mission, Share and Care runs a program in Chennai and also a village in Tiruvallur, where the 400 elderly live. This program ensures that every month, the 400 senior citizens have dry food rations, medicines and medical care, clothes, physiotherapy, geriatric counselling and other services to nurture them back to good physical health. Need for the fundraiserOne of the founders, Carmel Arokiasamy, shares that the abandoned elderly parents who live in extreme poverty without food or anyone to care for them constantly think about their children and ask, “Did we do something wrong?” Arokiasamy shares that from the time their children are born, they take care of them in such a special way. But when their children abandon them, they weep and cannot stop thinking about them. While dealing with this emotional distress, they are also suffering from hunger and disease. But cannot afford food and medicines. Donate now to help Share and Care provide the abandoned elderly with dry food rations, medical supplies, a medical and eye camp, counselling, clothes, blankets and recreational activities to live their lives with a positive outlook, despite their circumstances.Your donation can provide these elderly parents with their most basic and urgent needs. “Please support our work because this is for the elderly parents who are in great need. These grandparents are bedridden. They need care, love and support”.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest online donation platform, to support Share and Care in their mission to provide food, medicines, counselling and personal care to 400 abandoned grandparents living in poverty.

campaigns.title: Help Vinay bring children facing abuse in red-light areas to safety and protect them
campaigns.description: “I saw a girl, barely 7 or 8, being pushed into flesh trade. Her cries for help still echo in my ears. There were men as old as 60 r*ping the innocent child. That was the day I knew my mission in life was to get these children out of the hell-like conditions in red-light areas. They are nothing short of hell and no child deserves to grow up in this environment” says Vinay Vasta, founder of Social Activities Integration, an organisation committed to freeing children trapped in red-light areas.Vinay and his NGO have been able to provide education, medical care and nutrition to over 2,500 children. But his dream to provide safe shelter to children remains unrealized. “We do our best to keep these children safe during the day but sadly they are forced to go back to the red light areas at night as we don’t have a home for them. Your donation can help us build a safe home for children of sex workers and prevent them from being pulled into prostitution. Donate to make this dream a realityDonate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).Life in red light areas“S*x workers use their homes which are barely 6 feet by 4 feet for their business. They invite customers in the presence of their children. Their children not only witness abuse but experience it too. Just imagine a child being told that all she can ever become in life is a pr*stitute”, Vinay shares as he explains the extent of abuse children face in red light areas.This is a life that thousands of children are forced to live behind closed doors where they suffer abuse and exploitation morning, noon and night.A ray of hopeVinay has been relentlessly trying to give children the care and education they deserve. He has provided quality English education to thousands of children, enabling them to secure a livelihood out of red-light areas. Several children taught by Vinay are today leading successful lives as professionals and Government employees. However the sad reality is that Vinay and his NGO, in the absence of a permanent shelter home, are unable to provide round the clock safety to these children, which is essential for their well-being.Your donations can enable SAI to build a shelter home for the children of sex workers and usher in a new tomorrow for them, away from the horrors of red-light areas.Donate to this fundraiser on Give.do, India’s largest and most trusted online donation platform, to provide safe shelter for children of sex workers and save them from abuse and second generation pr*stitution.NGO's 80G number: AACTS0408EF20216NGO's 12A number: AACTS0408EE20028

campaigns.title: 691 People. 684 Children. One Shared Promise.
campaigns.description: 691 People. 684 Children. One Shared Promise.As India prepares for its Census, around 80 lakh migrant children will be living between two addresses.Between one village and another.Between one school and the next.For many children, migration does not simply mean changing homes. It means interrupted learning, delayed enrolment, missing records, and sometimes losing their connection to education altogether.Not because they lack potential.Not because they do not want to learn.But because no system consistently follows them when they move.Migration may be temporary. The education loss it causes can last a lifetime.Every year, thousands of children move with their families in search of work. As they cross districts and states, many slip through the cracks of the education system. A child who was enrolled in school yesterday may find themselves disconnected from learning tomorrow simply because their family migrated.A child's future should not depend on their postal address.At Learning Initiatives for India (LIFI), we are working to ensure that migration does not interrupt a child's education.Over the last six years, we have worked alongside migrant communities to understand migration patterns, support children's learning journeys, and build systems that help children remain visible to the education system wherever they go.This year, we aim to support 684 migrant children through their migration journeys.Our work focuses on identifying where children migrate, tracking their movement across locations, connecting with organisations and communities that can support them at destination sites, and ensuring they remain connected to learning despite relocation.What Success Looks LikeFor us, success is not measured by the number of meetings conducted or records collected.Success is a child who continues learning despite migration.By the end of this campaign, we will share:• How many children were successfully tracked during migration.• How many children were connected to support systems at destination locations.• How many children remained connected to education despite relocation.Because every child who remains in education is one less child lost in transition.Why This MattersWhen a child migrates, the challenge is not simply moving from one place to another. The challenge is ensuring that the child does not disappear from the education system in the process.Education is one of the few pathways that can break cycles of poverty and vulnerability. Yet for migrant children, access to education is often determined by mobility rather than potential.We believe this is not just an education challenge. It is a matter of justice.No child should lose access to learning because their family had to move in search of work.How Your Support HelpsSupporting migrant children requires more than a one-time intervention.Your contribution helps us build and sustain a system that follows children through migration, strengthens partnerships across locations, supports community-based responses, and enables continuous tracking of children's educational journeys.Funds raised through this campaign will help us:• Map and track children's migration journeys.• Connect children with organisations and communities at destination locations.• Strengthen systems and technology that support educational continuity.• Enable teams to coordinate, monitor, and follow up with children throughout the migration cycle.Together, these efforts help ensure that migration does not become a reason for educational exclusion.One Shared PromiseTo make this possible, we need to raise ₹20,02,000.We are looking for 691 individuals to contribute approximately ₹2,900 each.A child's address may change.Their education shouldn't.Your contribution can help ensure that a migrant child remains connected to learning, no matter where their journey takes them.Contribute today. Help keep a child connected to education.

campaigns.title: Support an EWS Special Needs Inclusive School – Build 2 Classrooms for Children in Need
campaigns.description: Fundraising for the Healing Nest Foundation allows me to support children with special needs and help them access the care and opportunities they deserve. Through this initiative, children will receive nutritious meals, clothing, emotional support, and quality education.At Healing Nest Foundation, we run Titli School — an EWS Special Needs Inclusive School in New Delhi, designed for children who are often excluded from mainstream education systems.These are children who:Come from Economically Weaker Sections (EWS)Have special needs or developmental challengesStruggle to adapt in a conventional school environment.Require emotional, behavioral, and learning supportFor many of them, Titli is not just a school — it is the first experience of being accepted, understood, and nurtured.🌱 Why We Need Your SupportAs an EWS Special Needs Inclusive School, we are seeing a growing number of children in need of a safe and supportive learning environment. However, we are currently limited by:A Lack of classroom spaceTo address this, we are working towards👉 Building 2 additional classroomsThis will help us:Admit more children who are currently on the waiting list.Create dedicated spaces for learning + therapyProvide a calm, sensory-friendly environmentStrengthen our inclusive education model🧩 What Makes This School DifferentTitli School is not a conventional educational setup.As an inclusive school for EWS children special needs and out-of-school children we:Combine education, therapy, and emotional developmentAllow children to learn at their own paceFocus on confidence, communication, and essential life skillsEnsure every child is treated with dignity, patience and care.For many families, this becomes the only space where their child truly feels a sense of belonging.❤️ The Impact Your Contribution will directly :Therapy & early years schooling for children with special needs.Construction of 2 new classroomsSafe learning opportunities for 100+ children every year.Structured support from therapists and educatorsExpansion of an inclusive education enviornment.🙏 Call to ActionEvery child deserves a space where they feel accepted, supported, and included.By supporting this EWS Special Needs Inclusive School, you are helping build classrooms, confidence, dignity, and a brighter future👉Support our mission. Share this initiative. Become part of their journey.” .

campaigns.title: Help abandoned elderly with shelter and care
campaigns.description: Every day, elderly parents are left alone on the streets, at train stations, or even outside temples. Many are sick, struggling with age-related problems, and have no money or family to turn to. Some suffer for weeks or months before anyone notices their pain. The heartbreaking truth? They were abandoned by their own families.Although Indian law requires children and relatives to care for senior citizens, many elders still face neglect, abuse, and abandonment. The problem is only getting worse.But there is hope. Our dedicated NGO partners work day and night to rescue these forgotten elders. They provide safe shelter, medical care, nutritious meals, and most importantly—love and respect. Through old age homes across the country, our partners give these seniors a chance to live their remaining years with dignity, comfort, and peace.You can make a difference. Your donation will help our NGO partners:Rescue homeless elders from the streetsProvide shelter, food, and medical careRestore their faith in humanity with kindness and supportNo elder should spend their final years alone and suffering. Your generosity can give them a fresh start. Donate today and help an abandoned senior citizen find safety, care, and hope.

campaigns.description: I am fundraising for a cause close to my heart, to bring a lasting change in the lives of the underprivileged.
